title: Iyogin HD and Ehime Bank to Merge to Form Regional Group with 12 Trillion Yen in Assets
summary: The two financial institutions in Ehime Prefecture agreed to integrate in April 2027 to strengthen their competitiveness in Japan's consolidated banking sector.

title: ECB Holds Interest Rates, Assesses Impact of Oil Price Rise and Inflation
summary: The European Central Bank decided to maintain its monetary policy amid the persistent energy shock and geopolitical uncertainty, though a possible hike is expected in September.

title: Yen Weakens to 163 Yen per Dollar, Lowest Level in Nearly 40 Years
summary: The Japanese currency continues its decline against the dollar due to divergent monetary policies and tensions in the Middle East, surpassing the 163 yen per dollar threshold.

title: Kōzō Okamoto, Former Japanese Red Army Member Convicted for Lod Airport Attack, Dies in Lebanon
summary: Okamoto, 75, the only survivor of the commando that attacked Lod Airport in 1972, died in Beirut where he had lived as a refugee for decades.

title: Unions Demand 75 Yen Increase in Minimum Wage while Employers Urge Caution
summary: Annual negotiations to set the minimum wage in Japan enter their final stretch with unions demanding a record increase, while companies warn of the impact on employment and SMEs.

title: European Union Imposes Record 166 Billion Yen Fine on Google for Abuse of Dominant Position
summary: The European Commission sanctioned the tech giant for favoring its own services in search results, the largest fine ever imposed on a company in the EU.

title: Fukuoka Assembly President at Center of Scandal over Alleged Fund Transfer to Prime Minister
summary: Katsuhiro Kurauchi met with Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi amid corruption suspicions, while the regional assembly faces multiple allegations that have damaged its credibility.

title: Bill for 'Alternative Capital' Stalls due to Lack of Agreement between Government and Opposition
summary: The initiative, which aims to create a backup administrative headquarters in case of emergency, cannot advance in the Diet due to disagreements over its scope and necessity.

title: Japanese and Chinese Foreign Ministers Hold First Direct Contact after Deterioration of Relations
summary: Japanese Foreign Minister Toshimitsu Motegi and his Chinese counterpart Wang Yi spoke briefly on the sidelines of a meeting in Laos without any concrete progress announced.

title: Go Oiwa to Take Over as New Japan Football Coach from Spring 2025
summary: The Japan Football Association confirmed that the coach, who led the Olympic team to the quarterfinals in Paris 2024, will replace Hajime Moriyasu after the Asian Cup.

title: Seven-Eleven Confirms Employee Hoarded and Resold Popular Character Merchandise
summary: The convenience store chain issued guidelines to prevent unauthorized resale after discovering that a worker bought large quantities of goods for subsequent resale at inflated prices.

title: Filmmakers Hirokazu Kore-eda and Shinya Tsukamoto to Compete at Venice Film Festival
summary: Both Japanese directors will present their latest works in the official section of the prestigious Italian festival, to be held in September.

title: Chinese Mathematician Wang Hong Wins Fields Medal, First Woman from Mainland China to Do So
summary: Wang is one of four recipients of the most important prize in mathematics, in an edition that also recognized two other researchers born in China.

title: United States and Saudi Arabia Sign Civil Nuclear Cooperation Agreement with Flexible Conditions
summary: The pact, which includes the transfer of atomic technology, raises concerns about possible nuclear proliferation in the region, according to critics.

title: Japan to Mandate Blending of Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF) at Seven Airports
summary: The government plans to require a minimum proportion of 5% SAF on international flights from 2030, with the aim of decarbonizing the aviation sector.

title: Sumo Kirishima Defeats Onosato and Takes First Place in Tournament
summary: On the eleventh day of the sumo tournament in Nagoya, Kirishima achieved his tenth victory by defeating yokozuna Onosato, tying for the lead with two other wrestlers.

title: Japan Suffers Intense Heatwave with Temperatures up to 41.1 Degrees and Dozens Hospitalized
summary: The Tokai region records several consecutive days of extreme heat, with at least one death from heat stroke and multiple emergencies, while high temperatures are expected to continue.

title: Businessman Arrested for Evading 360 Million Yen in Taxes through Fraudulent Use of 'Angel Tax'
summary: The 55-year-old suspect allegedly abused the tax incentive system for startups, hiding income and claiming improper deductions.

title: JAXA Successfully Completes Ground Combustion Test of Epsilon S Rocket Engine
summary: The test was conducted without incident at the Tanegashima Space Center, unlike two previous attempts that ended in explosion, marking progress in the solid-fuel rocket program.

title: Former Daito City Employee Arrested Again on Suspicion of Corruption in Public Contracts
summary: The former section chief allegedly received bribes totaling 8 million yen in exchange for favoring companies in the award of municipal works.

title: Houthis Attack Saudi Oil Tanker in Bab el-Mandeb Strait, Extending Conflict to Red Sea
summary: The drone attack on a vessel linked to Saudi Arabia raises tension on the vital sea route for crude oil trade, causing an increase in oil prices.

title: Woman Injured by Stray Bullet While Driving in Aichi Prefecture
summary: The victim, in her 50s, suffered ear injuries after being hit by a projectile from a hunting rifle that pierced the window of her light vehicle.

title: Prime Minister Modi Announces Special Courts to Try University Exam Fraud in India
summary: The opposition, led by the 'People's Cockroach Party', intensifies its criticism of the government over the scandal and demands the resignation of the Education Minister.

title: Princess Kiko Visits Wajima to Oversee Group Medical Checkups and Support Earthquake Victims
summary: The Japanese princess toured health facilities and participated in a meeting with mothers and children, showing concern for evacuees and recommending caution in the heat.
